ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for a combined onion rings and french fries combo product. FOODON_00002809 (french fries food product) is used as the closest parent anchor for the fries component; onion rings component lacks a dedicated FoodOn term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Potato products, fried) is the closest defensible parent for the fries component. No FoodEx2 code exists for a combined onion rings and fries combo. Onion rings are battered and deep-fried but fall under a separate vegetable-based fried product category not represented in FoodEx2 at this level.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 255620007 (French fries) is used as the closest parent for the fries component of this combo. No SNOMED CT code exists for a combined onion rings and fries combo product.

serving.description: 1 combo serving (onion rings portion + fries portion)
serving.natural_language_portion: One combo serving of approximately 300 g, comprising a portion of jumbo battered onion rings and a portion of seasoned french fries, as typically served at Potato Corner Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Onion rings, breaded, fried (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #35155) and French fries, frozen, oven-heated (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #11408 / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for this specific Potato Corner combo product. Nutrient values are estimated as a 50:50 blend by weight of (1) USDA SR Legacy entry for onion rings, breaded and fried (FDC ID 35155) and (2) USDA SR Legacy entry for french fries, frozen, oven-heated, salted (FDC ID 11408), which is the closest approved analogue for seasoned commercial fries. Values are per 100 g of the blended combo. This is a medium-confidence estimate; exact restaurant formulation and seasoning may alter values.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database entries for french fries and battered onion rings
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Potato Corner combo product. GI is estimated as a weighted average of (1) french fries GI ≈ 75 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr) and (2) battered onion rings GI ≈ 76 (analogous battered fried vegetable product, University of Sydney GI Database). Blended GI estimate: 75 (high). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (75 × 111 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 83.3. Available carbohydrate calculated as carbohydrate_g minus dietary_fibre_g per 100 g × 3 (serving size factor).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~930 mg per combo serving) from seasoning and batter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this combo, as a single 300 g serving provides approximately 930 mg sodium — nearly 40% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg — and the deep-fried preparation adds no potassium benefit to offset sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI ≈ 75) and very high glycemic load per serving (~83) from refined wheat batter and deep-fried potato
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this combo as an occasional indulgence only, as the high GI and very high glycemic load per serving (~83 GL units) will cause rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods or protein are strongly advised if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (~14 g/100 g), saturated fat (~2.4 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), and high sodium from deep-frying in vegetable oil with seasoning
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of this combo, as the combination of high total fat, saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium from deep-frying and seasoning contributes to atherogenic risk and elevated blood pressure; a 300 g serving provides approximately 42 g total fat and 930 mg sodium.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jumbo Onion Rings and French Fries Combo is a deep-fried Western-style snack combo served at Potato Corner outlets in Singapore, notably at the Funan mall location. The combo pairs two popular fried side dishes: jumbo-sized battered onion rings made from sweet onions coated in a crispy wheat flour batter and deep-fried to a golden colour, and seasoned french fries made from potato strips fried and dusted with a choice of flavoured seasoning powder. The onion rings deliver a crunchy exterior with a soft, sweet onion interior, while the fries offer a crispy, salty, and savoury profile. Both components are ultra-processed, deep-fried products with high energy density (~289 kcal per 100 g), high carbohydrate content (~37 g/100 g), and moderate-to-high fat content (~14 g/100 g). The glycemic index is high (GI ≈ 75), and a single 300 g combo serving carries a very high glycemic load (~83 GL units), making it clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. Sodium content is elevated (~310 mg/100 g; ~930 mg per serving) due to seasoning. The combo is vegetarian and typically halal-certified at Potato Corner Singapore. It is not suitable for gluten-free diets due to the wheat flour batter on the onion rings. This product is best consumed as an occasional treat and is not recommended as a regular dietary component for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific Potato Corner combo product in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central branded, or ICMR-NIN). Values are estimated as a 50:50 blend by weight of USDA SR Legacy entries for battered fried onion rings and frozen oven-heated french fries, which are the closest approved analogues. GI is estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogues for french fries and battered fried vegetable products. Confidence is medium due to the estimation approach and the fact that Potato Corner's proprietary seasoning blends and exact frying conditions are unknown.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a 50:50 blend of USDA SR Legacy FDC #35155 (onion rings, breaded, fried) and USDA SR Legacy FDC #11408 (french fries, frozen, oven-heated, salted). Exact restaurant formulation may differ. 2. Serving size of 300 g is an estimate for a jumbo combo; actual weight may vary by restaurant. 3. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based fried product with no animal-derived ingredients contributing B12; confirmed zero per USDA source data for both component foods. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries for either component food. 5. trans_fat_g (~0.1 g/100 g) reflects naturally occurring trans fats from frying oil; this is a low but non-zero value per USDA data. 6. Halal status is based on Potato Corner Singapore's general halal certification; individual outlet certification should be verified. 7. GI estimate of 75 is based on University of Sydney GI Database analogues for french fries (GI 75) and battered fried onion rings (GI ~76); blended estimate rounded to 75. 8. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type as no exact ontology term exists for a combined onion rings and fries combo product. 9. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-served food product. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy blended values and are provided as best-effort approximations.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with USDA SR Legacy blended nutrient estimates, University of Sydney GI analogous estimate, three ontology closest_parent anchors, full Singaporean multilingual naming, and comprehensive aliases.

Iteration 2: Addressed Q8 failure from previous iteration. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Corrected a Chinese Traditional character discrepancy ('洋蔥圈薯条' to '洋蔥圈薯條') and added the romanized alias 'Da Fen Yang Cong Quan Shu Tiao Tao Can' to the flat aliases list, ensuring all strings from aliases_by_language are present verbatim in aliases. Verified vitamin_b12_mcg zero justification in notes. Verified vitamin_d_mcg -1 sentinel documentation in notes. Verified all ontology match_type values use only permitted strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed GI is numeric > 0. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 criteria confirmed passing.
